stimulus.content: Alexander Hamilton and Thomas Jefferson had different views on how the country should be run. Hamilton wanted a strong national bank and supported industry. Jefferson wanted a nation of farmers and believed states should hold more power. Their disagreements led to the first political parties.

stem: Which TWO ideas did Alexander Hamilton support for the new nation?

choices.text: Keeping the nation focused on farming
Giving more power to state governments
Building up American industry and trade

answer_explanation: The passage states Hamilton wanted a strong national bank and supported industry. Jefferson, not Hamilton, favored farming and stronger state power.
